name: Trilho Azul — circular route Parque Florestal de Monsanto
name_original: Trilho Azul — percurso circular Parque Florestal de Monsanto
region_intro: Jogging routes around Campolide offer access to a variety of green spaces and urban parks within Lisbon. The area is characterized by its proximity to expansive forest parks like Monsanto, providing extensive trails and natural surroundings. Runners can also find well-maintained paths in more formal settings such as Parque Eduardo VII, which features wide loops and some hilly sections. This blend of urban and natural environments creates diverse running conditions, from flat park paths to more challenging forest trails. Best jogging routes around Campolide The most popular jogging route is Trilho Azul — circular route Parque Florestal de Monsanto, a 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail that takes 49 minutes to complete. This moderate route winds through the largest green area in Lisbon, offering a natural escape. Another top favourite among local runners is Aqueduto das Águas Livres – Trail in the Woods loop from Campolide, a difficult 6.2 miles (10.0 km) path. This route starts directly from Campolide and leads through wooded areas, providing varied terrain. Local runners also love the Small Lake loop from São Sebastião da Pedreira, a 2.5 miles (4.1 km) trail leading through a local park, often completed in about 27 minutes. Jogging around Campolide is defined by expansive forest parks, urban green spaces, and scenic viewpoints over Lisbon. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy park loops to more challenging forest trails. The routes in Campolide are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 30 reviews. Almost 1000 runners have used komoot to explore Campolide's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes can I find around Campolide?
A: There are over 25 dedicated running routes around Campolide, offering a wide variety of options for different preferences and fitness levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars.

Q: Are there easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Campolide?
A: Yes, Campolide offers several easy jogging routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed run. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32337058" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small Lake loop from São Sebastião da Pedreira&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.5-mile (4.1 km) trail that can be completed in about 27 minutes, leading through a pleasant local park.

Q: Where can I find longer or more challenging running trails near Campolide?
A: For longer or more challenging runs, you should explore the trails within Parque Florestal Monsanto, Lisbon's largest green area. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23676520" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aqueduto das Águas Livres – Trail in the Woods loop from Campolide&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 6.2-mile (10.0 km) path that starts directly from Campolide and takes you through varied wooded terrain.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Campolide area?
A: Absolutely. Many of the routes around Campolide are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381163802/trilho-azul-circular-route-parque-florestal-de-monsanto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trilho Azul — circular route Parque Florestal de Monsanto&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 4.3-mile (6.9 km) trail that winds through the expansive Monsanto Forest Park.

Q: What kind of natural features or viewpoints can I expect along the running paths?
A: Jogging routes around Campolide offer a mix of natural beauty and urban vistas. You can enjoy extensive forest trails in Monsanto Park, or wide paths with panoramic views of Lisbon, including Avenida da Liberdade, São Jorge Castle, and the Tagus River, from Parque Eduardo VII. Specific viewpoints include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8178196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eduardo VII Park Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter lakes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5883754" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake of Parque da Paz&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6444956" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amália Rodrigues Garden Lake&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the running trails around Campolide suitable for families?
A: Yes, many of the parks and green spaces near Campolide are family-friendly. Parque Eduardo VII, with its wide paths, and some of the smaller local parks like Jardim da Bela-Flor or Jardins de Campolide, provide safe and accessible environments for families to enjoy a run or walk together. Look for easy-rated routes for the most family-friendly options.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Campolide?
A: Many green spaces in Lisbon, including parts of Parque Florestal Monsanto, are generally dog-friendly, especially on leash. It's always a good idea to check local regulations for specific parks, but the natural environment of the larger parks makes them suitable for running with your canine companion.

Q: What are the terrain and elevation like on Campolide's running routes?
A: The terrain varies significantly. You'll find flat, wide paths in areas like the central walk of Parque Eduardo VII, while other trails within the park and especially in Parque Florestal Monsanto offer more varied terrain with some hilly sections and natural forest paths. This blend provides options for different fitness levels and preferences.

Q: Is there public transport access to the running areas from Campolide?
A: Campolide is well-connected by public transport, making it easy to access nearby running spots. Parque Eduardo VII is centrally located and easily accessible, and Monsanto Park also has various access points that can be reached via public transport from Campolide. Lisbon's infrastructure is generally pedestrian-friendly, aiding access to these green spaces.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or monuments to see while running?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can encounter several notable landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/399846" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to the Overseas Combatants&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6947921" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alameda Luminous Fountain&lt;/a&gt; are examples of man-made monuments that might be near or visible from some routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/215599" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Christ the King&lt;/a&gt; also offers a prominent sight in the distance from various viewpoints.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Campolide?
A: The running routes around Campolide are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, from urban park loops to extensive forest trails, and the beautiful scenic views, especially those overlooking Lisbon and the Tagus River. The accessibility to large green spaces within the city is also a frequently highlighted positive aspect.
tips:
Parque Eduardo VII
This famous park is named after British King Edward VII who visited the city in the twentieth century. Parque Eduardo VII is the largest park in the center of Lisbon. The park is built against a hill.
The panoramico is temporarily closed (May 2024), no one was working so it will remain closed for a while........
This point in Monsanto Park offers an unpretentious view of Lisbon. It becomes convenient as a stopping point in the middle of the race.

waypoints.name: Miradouro do Moinho das Três Cruzes
View of the Águas Livres Aqueduct and the city of Lisbon
